evals.prompt: what unmerged branches do we have? help me figure out which ones to clean up and which to keep
evals.expected_output: An interactive HTML dashboard served locally with branch cards, safety verdicts, action dropdowns, and cleanup scripts. Should use the skill's data gathering steps and produce a Vue 3 page.
expectations: Output is a single HTML file
HTML uses Vue 3 (cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vue@3 or similar CDN)
Dashboard has tab navigation (All, Delete, Work Queue or Next Up, Script)
Each branch has an action selector (delete, keep, next-up, park, etc.)
Branch cards show PR status information (merged, open, closed, or no-pr)
Safety verdicts are shown (verified-safe, likely-safe, has-unique-work, or needs-review)
Delete tab includes generated git cleanup scripts (git branch -D or git push origin --delete)
Dashboard uses dark theme styling
Branch data is embedded directly in the HTML (not loaded from external file)

evals.prompt: I want to review my branches on my phone later tonight - give me something I can paste into Claude on my phone to get a triage dashboard
evals.expected_output: Should run the bundled gather_branch_data.py script and present the prompt output for copying. Should mention the template.html for rendering the AI's JSON response.
expectations: Ran the bundled script gather_branch_data.py (or equivalent data gathering)
Output includes a prompt/text that can be pasted into another AI
The prompt asks the AI to respond with JSON (not HTML)
References the template.html file for rendering the JSON response
The prompt includes branch metadata (names, PR status, commit counts)
The prompt includes a JSON schema or example showing the expected output format
Does NOT generate a full HTML dashboard (this is the offline workflow)

evals.prompt: show me which branches are safe to delete. I want to clean up stale stuff but I'm worried about losing work since we squash merge
evals.expected_output: Should emphasize squash-merge-aware safety verification. Should produce a dashboard or report with safety verdicts (verified-safe, likely-safe, etc.) and confidence scores. Should NOT use hash-based ancestry checks.
expectations: Output addresses squash merge concerns explicitly
Uses content-based verification (git diff, not git merge-base --is-ancestor)
Safety verdicts are assigned to branches (verified-safe, likely-safe, has-unique-work, needs-review)
Safety detail explanations are provided for each branch
Branches with verified-safe or likely-safe verdicts are marked for deletion
Branches with has-unique-work are NOT defaulted to delete
Generated cleanup scripts only pre-select safe-to-delete branches
Output is an HTML dashboard (not just text)
